description: Expo pins the exact version of every native module for an SDK release (see
PRs that pass CI — jest never touches native code — while moving the app onto versions Expo
does not sanction, which `npx expo install --check` then reports as broken.
These move only when the SDK moves: bump `expo` itself, then run `npx expo install --check`
and `npx expo install --fix` to take whatever the new SDK expects.
`react-test-renderer` is here for the same reason by a different route: it must match the
`react` version exactly, and react is pinned by Expo.
Major upgrades of the toolchain are migrations, not dependency bumps — TypeScript 7, ESLint
10, Tailwind 4, Jest 30 and Zod 4 each need code changes. Surface them on the dashboard and
let a human opt in, rather than opening PRs that sit red or, worse, green and wrong.

packageRules.description: Group the low-risk dev tooling into a single PR so the queue stays short.

description: release-please and the Play publishing chain are load-bearing for shipping: a silent
behaviour change there stops releases without failing a build. Keep them opt-in.
